message:
fix(lint): preserve duplicate ids when fetching issues in resolved order

fetch_issues_in_resolved_order() built a HashMap<String, Issue> from
get_issues_by_ids() and then called HashMap::remove(id) for each id
in resolved_ids. Because remove() yanks the entry out of the map, a
second occurrence of the same id in resolved_ids (which is legal —
the caller may pass the same id twice when batching) returned an
IssueNotFound error rather than the previously fetched issue.

Replace the .remove(id) call with .get(id).cloned() so each id can be
looked up arbitrarily many times. Issue is small and Clone is already
required upstream, so the per-clone cost is negligible.

Add a regression test that creates a single issue, calls the helper
with the same id listed twice, and asserts both slots in the returned
Vec are populated with the issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
